forecast-period.text: A high pressure system over southern WA will move steadily eastwards over the next several days to be over SA on Friday and Saturday, reaching NSW on Sunday and the Tasman Sea by Monday. Northerly winds freshen over the state on Tuesday ahead of a mid-week change.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Isolated fog patches in the west. Showers about the west coast and agricultural areas, mainly about windward coasts and ranges. Maximum temperatures near to slightly below average. Southwest to southerly winds about the agricultural area, becoming light in the northwest.

Isolated fog patches in the west. Light showers about the southern agricultural area, contracting to southern coasts during the afternoon and evening. Maximum temperatures near average. West to northwesterly winds in the south, tending light easterly in the far north.

Isolated fog patches about central and eastern districts. Isolated showers mainly about the southern coasts and ranges mostly clear in during the evening. Maximum temperatures near to slightly above average. Light winds tending northeast to northerly during the day.

text.p: Fine apart from isolated showers developing about the far west late evening. Maximum temperatures above average. Winds becoming northeast to northwesterly during the day.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Monday: Less than 2 mm, increasing to area of 2 to 10 mm about the southwest Flinders district and the southern coasts and ranges, possibly reaching 5 to 15 mm about the Limestone coast.
